findings.title: uv.lock out of sync with declared version bounds
findings.problem: The tracked uv.lock file specifies pytest 9.0.3 and pytest-cov 7.1.0, which violate the declared upper bounds in requirements.txt (pytest<9, pytest-cov<6) and pyproject.toml[dev]. The lock file was last updated in pass-2 (2026-06-13 09:31:50) before pytest bounds were tightened in pass-3 (2026-06-13 10:31:21). Anyone using 'uv install' to bootstrap the environment would install versions that break CI/prod parity — the core principle established in passes 2-11 that CI and prod must exercise the same major versions.
findings.best_practice_violated: CI/prod parity principle (pass-2 #191, pass-8 #264, pass-11 final note): locked dependencies must enforce the same version bounds across all installation mechanisms (pip, uv, etc.). A tracked lock file that violates the source-of-truth bounds creates a silent divergence path.
findings.proposed_fix: Either: (1) Update uv.lock to resolve pytest to 8.x and pytest-cov to 5.x by running 'uv sync' and committing the result, or (2) Remove uv.lock from version control and document that pip/requirements-ci.txt is the canonical installation path for CI and development. Option 1 is preferred if uv is a supported installation method; option 2 if pip is the sole supported mechanism.

findings.title: OTel context not propagated in _run_sync worker thread path
findings.problem: When _run_sync executes in an already-running event loop (line 220), it submits asyncio.run(coro) to _submit_bounded without propagating the OTel context. This contrasts with _with_timeout (lines 185-208), which explicitly captures ctx = otel_context.get_current() and re-attaches it in the worker via otel_context.attach/detach. Verified defect: submitting to worker without context attachment means baggage (run_id) is unavailable in the worker thread; any instrumentation or logging that reads baggage in the T3/asyncio path will receive None instead of the run_id. The pass-3 fix (#238) applied this pattern to _with_timeout but _run_sync was not updated despite handling the same thread-pool execution path.
findings.best_practice_violated: Consistent OTel context propagation across thread boundaries (established pattern from _with_timeout); baggage/run_id accessibility for instrumentation in worker threads
findings.proposed_fix: Mirror the _with_timeout pattern: before _submit_bounded, capture ctx = otel_context.get_current(), then wrap the lambda to attach/detach context in the worker. Lines 210-220 become: ctx = otel_context.get_current(); def _run(): token = otel_context.attach(ctx); try: return asyncio.run(coro); finally: otel_context.detach(token); return _submit_bounded(_run).result(...). This ensures baggage is accessible in the T3 execution and future instrumentation is correctly linked.

result.title: uv.lock out of sync with declared version bounds (pytest, pytest-cov)
result.verification: Verified through code inspection: /Users/thomasjones/hc-grc/uv.lock contains pytest 9.0.3 (line 317) and pytest-cov 7.1.0 (line 333), which violate declared bounds in requirements.txt:100-101 (pytest>=8.2.2,<9, pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6), pyproject.toml:41-42 (same bounds in [dev]), and requirements-ci.txt:31-32 (same). uv.lock was created once in pass-2 (commit 2027513, 2026-06-13 09:31:50) and never updated; bounds were tightened in pass-11 (commit 6debed1, 2026-06-15). Testing with 'uv sync --dry-run' confirms it would install pytest==9.0.3 and pytest-cov==7.1.0. However, severity is LOW because: (1) CI explicitly uses 'pip install -r requirements-ci.txt' per .github/workflows/test.yml:51, not uv; (2) project documentation (README.md, CONTRIBUTING_DOCS.md, etc.) never mentions uv as a supported installation method; (3) uv.lock is tracked in git but there is no documented pathway instructing users to use it. The defect is REAL (violates CI/prod parity principle) but has minimal practical impact due to lack of documented uv usage.
result.recommended_action: Update uv.lock to resolve pytest to >=8.2.2,<9 and pytest-cov to >=5.0.0,<6 by running 'uv sync' in the repository and committing the result. This ensures consistency across all three installation mechanisms (pip/requirements-ci.txt, pip/pyproject.toml[dev], and uv/uv.lock), maintaining the CI/prod parity principle established in pass-2. Alternatively, remove uv.lock from version control and document that pip with requirements.txt or requirements-ci.txt is the canonical installation method, if uv is not a supported installation path going forward. Option 1 is preferred for consistency.

result.title: OTel context not propagated in _run_sync worker thread path
result.verification: Read src/reasoning_client.py:210-220 (_run_sync), 185-208 (_with_timeout), 328-423 (_t3_async), 426-453 (_t3_complete), and 528-556 (complete() baggage read). Traced the execution flow: (1) complete() reads baggage at lines 530-535 and stores in run_id variable BEFORE calling _attempt(); (2) the OTel span is created with that extracted run_id at lines 552-554; (3) _t3_async (the coroutine passed to _run_sync) contains zero baggage or otel_context reads—it only drains messages from the SDK's query(); (4) the SDK runs in a subprocess via ClaudeAgentOptions, so thread-local context wouldn't reach it anyway. The candidate claim assumes baggage must be available in the worker thread, but baggage is read and extracted in the main thread (in complete()) before submission, and _t3_async has no code path that depends on OTel context. The _with_timeout pattern (pass-3 #238) was added for T2/Ollama LangChain spans to nest properly under reasoning.complete—a different requirement. No realistic scenario where (a) complete() is called from a running event loop AND (b) instrumentation in the worker actually needs OTel context. Test test_run_sync_with_running_loop artificially injects a loop but the app's LangGraph execution is synchronous.
result.recommended_action: No action needed. This is not a live defect. The baggage/run_id mechanism is already working correctly: it is read in complete() before any worker submission, the span carries the run_id, and _t3_async has no code that depends on OTel context being in the worker thread. The _with_timeout context-propagation pattern was specific to T2/Ollama instrumentation (LangChain spans), not to T3 (which runs in a subprocess). If in future development async node functions are added that call complete() from within an event loop, this could become relevant—but it is not a current reachable defect.
